name: Risotto with Cheese
recipeIngredient: 5 1/2 cups beef, chicken, or vegetable stock
1 1/2 cups medium grain risotto rice (such as arborio)
3/4 cup freshly grated Parmesan or Romano cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the stock in a medium saucepan and keep it simmering until needed.
2. In a large heavy saucepan, melt two-thirds of the butter. Stir in the onion and cook gently until soft and golden.
3. Add the rice, mixing well to coat with butter. After 1-2 minutes, pour in the wine.
4. Raise the heat slightly and cook until the wine evaporates. Add one small ladleful of hot stock.
5. Over moderate heat, cook until the stock is absorbed or evaporates, stirring the rice with a wooden spoon to prevent sticking.
6. Add more stock and stir until the rice dries out again. Continue stirring and adding liquid a little at a time.
7. After about 20 minutes of cooking, taste the rice. Add salt and pepper as needed.
8. Cook until the rice is al dente (tender but still firm to the bite). Total cooking time may be from 20-35 minutes.
9. If out of stock, use hot water, but do not worry if the rice is done before using all the liquid.
10. Remove pan from heat. Let sit in remaining butter and cheese for a few minutes.
11. Taste again for seasoning before serving. Allow risotto to rest 3-4 minutes before serving.
